env_axis.name: cohort-year
env_axis.shift_mechanism: cohort-year shift is compound: curriculum + platform + population each differ across years

facts: 2009/2012/2015/2017 cohorts; US middle/high school math; cross-cohort year-shift canonical OOD.
hypotheses: Cohort-year shift is primarily curriculum shift (math standards changed) rather than platform or population shift, because standards revisions over 2009-2017 alter the skill taxonomy directly.
Method ranking on 2009→2012 predicts ranking on 2012→2015, because the cohort-shift mechanism is stable across adjacent time-bins.
intuitions: Foundation-model work using ASSISTments data likely exists.
CSEDM workshop benchmarks for 2024-25 probably include these cohorts.
unknowns: Each cohort is a different enrolled school set, not a longitudinal panel, so whether year-as-environment conflates time with school cannot be determined from anonymized data.
Documented data-quality fixes (gameable answers, bugs) differ across cohorts, so cohort-as-environment is confounded with data-quality-fix history.
epistemic_state.boundary: The dataset covers K-12 US math only; it is smaller than EdNet but has a richer multi-year design.
